title: Glass article having a metallic nanofilm and method for manufacturing the glass article
abstract: An article including a glass or glass ceramic substrate, a noble metal layer, an adhesion promoting layer positioned between and bonded to the substrate and the noble metal layer, and a conductive metal layer positioned on and bonded to the noble metal layer. The adhesion promoting layer includes a siloxy group bonded with the substrate and a thiol group bonded to the noble metal layer. A method for manufacturing an article including applying an adhesion promoting layer comprising mercaptosilane to at least a portion of a glass or glass ceramic substrate, wherein siloxane bonds are formed between the mercaptosilane and the substrate, applying a noble metal layer to the adhesion promoting layer, the noble metal layer bonds with a thiol present in the mercaptosilane, thermally treating the noble metal layer, and applying a conductive metal layer to the noble metal layer.
claims: Claims What is claimed is: 1. An article comprising: a glass or glass ceramic substrate; a noble metal layer; an adhesion promoting layer positioned between and bonded to the glass or glass ceramic substrate and the noble metal layer; and an electroplated conductive metal layer positioned directly on and bonded to the noble metal layer, wherein the adhesion promoting layer comprises a siloxy group bonded with the glass or glass ceramic substrate and a thiol group bonded to the noble metal layer. 2. The article of claim 1 , wherein the noble metal layer comprises a noble metal selected from the group consisting of copper, silver, and gold. 3. The article of any one of claims 1 and 2, wherein the noble metal layer consists essentially of silver. 4. The article of any one of claims 1 to 3, wherein the electroplated conductive metal layer is a conductive metal selected from the group consisting of copper, nickel, cobalt, gold, silver, cadmium, chromium, lead, platinum, and combinations and alloys thereof. 5. The article of any one of claims 1 to 4, wherein the electroplated conductive metal layer consists essentially of copper. 6. The article of any one of claims 1 to 5, wherein the adhesion promoting layer is a monolayer. 7. The article of any one of claims 1 to 6, wherein the adhesion promoting layer has a thickness of less than 100 nm. 8. The article of any one of claims 1 to 7, wherein the noble metal layer has a thickness of less than one micron. 9. The article of any one of claims 1 to 8, wherein the noble metal layer has a thickness from greater than or equal to 100 nm to less than or equal to 500 nm. 10. The article of any one of claims 1 to 9, wherein the noble metal layer is made from noble metal particles having an average particle size of less than or equal to 100 nm. 11. The article of any one of claims 1 to 10, wherein the noble metal layer is made from noble metal particles having an average particle size from greater than or equal to 1 nm to less than or equal to 50 nm. 12. The article of any one of claims 1 to 11 , wherein the noble metal layer has a sheet resistance of less than 100 ohm/square. 13. The article of any one of claims 1 to 12, wherein the glass or glass ceramic substrate comprises vias and/or trenches and the adhesion promoting layer, the noble metal layer, and the electroplated conductive metal layer are present in the vias and/or trenches. 14. The article of any one of claims 1 to 13, wherein an adherence of the electroplated conductive metal layer to the article is such that it passes a tape test at 3.0 N/cm. 15. A method for manufacturing an article comprising: applying an adhesion promoting layer comprising mercaptosilane to at least a portion of a glass or glass ceramic substrate, wherein siloxane bonds are formed between the mercaptosilane and the glass or glass ceramic substrate; applying a noble metal layer to the adhesion promoting layer, wherein the noble metal layer bonds with a thiol present in the mercaptosilane; thermally treating the noble metal layer by heating and cooling the noble metal layer; and electroplating a conductive metal layer directly to the noble metal layer. 16. The method of claim 15, wherein the noble metal layer comprises a noble metal selected from the group consisting of copper, silver, and gold. 17. The method of any one of claims 15 and 16, wherein the noble metal layer comprises silver. 18. The method of any one of claims 15 to 17, wherein the conductive metal layer is a conductive metal selected from the group consisting of copper, nickel, cobalt, gold, silver, cadmium, chromium, lead, platinum, and combinations and alloys thereof. 19. The method of any one of claims 15 to 18, wherein the conductive metal layer is copper. 20. The method of any one of claims 15 to 19, wherein the noble metal layer has a thickness of less than one micron. 21. The method of any one of claims 15 to 20, wherein the noble metal layer comprises noble metal particles having an average particle size of less than or equal to 100 nm. 22. The method of any one of claims 15 to 21, wherein thermally treating the noble metal layer comprises heating the noble metal to a temperature from greater than or equal to 150 Â°C to less than or equal to 700 Â°C and cooling to room temperature. 23. The method of any one of claims 15 to 22, wherein thermally treating the noble metal layer comprises heating the noble metal to a temperature from greater than or equal to 150 Â°C to less than or equal to 350 Â°C and cooling to room temperature. 24. The method of any one of claims 15 to 23, wherein the conductive metal layer is applied to the noble metal layer by electroplating. 25. The method of claim 24, wherein the noble metal layer is used as an electrode for electroplating the conductive metal layer to the noble metal layer. 26. The method of any one of claims 15 to 25, wherein an additional metal layer is applied to the noble metal layer by electroless plating, and the conductive metal layer is applied to the additional metal layer by electroplating. 27. The method of claim 26, wherein the additional metal layer comprises a member selected from the group consisting of copper, nickel, and cobalt. 28. The method of claim 26, wherein the additional metal layer consists essentially of copper.
